title: Upon Blanch. Epig.
content:
I have seen many maidens to have hair,
    Both for their comely need and some to spare;
    But Blanch has not so much upon her head
    As to bind up her chaps when she is dead.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Upon Blanch. Epig."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
